world.title: Caritas Delegates in Africa Pledge to Safeguard Church Identity, Resist “ideological colonization”
world.description: In a weeklong marking 20 years of Pope Benedict XVI’s 2005 Encyclical on Christian love, Deus Caritas Est (“God is Love”), delegates of Caritas Africa who gathered in Abidjan, Ivory Coast, from March 16 to 21 have pledged to safeguard the identity of Caritas as the Catholic Church’s humanitarian arm, warning against what they have described as “institutional drift and ideological colonization.”

world.title: How SECAM Report on Challenge of Polygamy Expands Pastoral Debate Begun at Synod on Synodality
world.description: The final report on the pastoral challenge of polygamy in Africa issued on Tuesday, March 24, offers a more structured theological and pastoral framework on the issue that has been under discussion since the multi-year XVI Ordinary General Assembly of the Synod of Bishops, the Synod on Synodality, called for discernment on the practice.

world.title: Scholar Calls for More Studies into Catholicism in Sierra Leone, Says Catholic Traditions Overlooked
world.description: In the writing of Sierra Leone’s history, the experience of the Catholic Church is often overlooked, a leading historian has said and called for independent studies into the growth of Catholicism in the West African nation.

world.title: “He was more than ordinary”: Tributes Pour in for Mill Hill Missionary Who Transformed Lives in Africa
world.description: Tributes are pouring in for Fr. Fons Eppink, a Dutch-born member of St. Joseph’s Missionaries Society (Mill Hill Missionaries/MHM), who served in several African countries, transforming lives especially through journalism and the formation of Priests.

Designed to accelerate the evolution of Japan's utility-scale BESS market, these high-performance systems address the increasingly stringent requirements of grid stability and multi-market participation.

science.title: Study on the influence of key parameters of sand emission on dust flux based on multi-source data
science.description: This study analyzes ground-based observations and multi-source remote sensing data from eight dust storm events in 2024 at two sites: the Tazhong (TZ) station in the Taklamakan Desert interior and the Xiaotang (XT) station on its northern margin, systematically investigates the interrelationships among dust particle size, friction velocity (U*), and dust flux, and evaluates the applicability of remote sensing data in dust monitoring. The results indicate that particle size significantly influences both horizontal fluxes (Q) and vertical dust fluxes (F). Fine particles (d[0.5]) enhance surface dust flux, while coarse particles (D[4,3]—due to their greater gravitational settling—are less capable of sustained suspension, limiting their long-distance transport. A positive correlation exists between friction velocity (U*) and Q, whereas its impact on F is weaker, suggesting that vertical transport is regulated primarily by particle size, gravitational settling, and turbulent structures. Regarding remote sensing data, MODIS Aerosol Optical Depth (AOD) shows strong consistency with ground-based dust flux measurements, especially at the Xiaotang (XT) station, where AOD closely follows the variation trends of both Q and F. This reflects the effectiveness of remote sensing data in capturing changes in dust activity. Additionally, the Aerosol Absorbing Index (AAI) from Sentinel-5P exhibits a highly significant positive correlation with ground-level dust concentrations, effectively reflecting the vertical structure of dust events. This research provides valuable data support and theoretical foundation for dust warning systems and desertification control projects.

science.title: Scientist Based in the U.S. Develops Biologically Intelligent Molecule That Identifies and Destroys Cancer Cells
science.description: Researcher at Harvard Medical School develops AI-driven molecule targeting tumor mitochondria defects to destroy cancer cells without harming healthy tissue.

science.title: Microtubules unveiled as active enzyme regulators
science.description: Microtubules, the dynamic filaments that form the cell's internal scaffolding, have long been viewed as mere passive structural supports. But a new study reveals they play a far more active signaling role. The findings, published in Science Advances, demonstrate that microtubules are in fact regulators of enzymatic reactions through reshaping the geometry of the enzyme's substrate proteins attached to them and controlling when key events occur to conduct cell division.

science.title: Study: Human-Driven Climate Change Slows Down Earth's Rotation
science.description: In recent years, climate change has played an increasingly significant role in altering the Earth's rotation.

science.title: Humans Return to Deep Space
science.description: NASA is all set to launch the Artemis 2 mission on April 1, 2026 and it will be marking the first crewed mission to beyond low Earth orbit in over 50 years. This mission will send the four astronauts on a 10-day journey around the Moon. And it also try to reviving human deep-space exploration after the era of Apollo 17. The launch represents milestone the NASA's Artemis program which is aimed at returning humans to the Moon.

science.title: The molecular mechanism that turns cool temps into nerve signals
science.description: When you reach into a bucket of ice, open your front door on a snowy day, or feel the tingle of menthol toothpaste, a protein in your nerve cells called TRPM8 springs into action, opening like a tiny gate to send a "cold" signal to your brain.

ai.title: Outlook Planet C3 2026: Climate Circularity Is the New Corporate Strategy. Are Companies Ready?
ai.description: Corporate climate action is entering a new phase as AI, policy frameworks and circular economy strategies reshape governance and capital allocation. With India strengthening its 2035 NDC targets, the Outlook Planet C3 Summit will bring policymakers and industry leaders together to accelerate climate resilience and sustainable growth.

ai.title: What Is SuperGrok Lite? Elon Musk’s Affordable AI Plan for Everyday Users

ai.title: ‘Most people don’t enjoy their jobs’: Perplexity CEO explains why losing jobs is okay
ai.description: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas sees AI job displacement as an opportunity for individuals to start their own businesses or learn AI and contribute to new companies. Despite some AI-related layoffs, many are already using AI for side hustles. Srinivas encourages embracing the future of AI rather than fearing it.
